title: DAISS: Phase-Aware Imitation Learning for Dual-Arm Robotic Ultrasound-Guided Interventions
summary: Imitation learning has shown strong potential for automating complex robotic manipulation. In medical robotics, ultrasound-guided needle insertion demands precise bimanual coordination, as clinicians must simultaneously manipulate an ultrasound probe to maintain an optimal acoustic view while steering an interventional needle. Automating this asymmetric workflow -- and reliably transferring expert strategies to robots -- remains highly challenging. In this paper, we present the Dual-Arm Interventional Surgical System (DAISS), a teleoperated platform that collects high-fidelity dual-arm demonstrations and learns a phase-aware imitation policy for ultrasound-guided interventions. To avoid constraining the operator's natural behavior, DAISS uses a flexible NDI-based leader interface for teleoperating two coordinated follower arms. To support robust execution under real-time ultrasound feedback, we develop a lightweight, data-efficient imitation policy. Specifically, the policy incorporates a phase-aware architecture and a dynamic mask loss tailored to asymmetric bimanual control. Conditioned on a planned trajectory, the network fuses real-time ultrasound with external visual observations to generate smooth, coordinated dual-arm motions. Experimental results show that DAISS can learn personalized expert strategies from limited demonstrations. Overall, these findings highlight the promise of phase-aware imitation-learning-driven dual-arm robots for improving precision and reducing cognitive workload in image-guided interventions.
tldr: The paper introduces DAISS, a teleoperated dual-arm robotic system for ultrasound-guided needle insertion, using phase-aware imitation learning to automate and personalize expert strategies for precise bimanual coordination.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了DAISS，一种用于超声引导针插入的遥操作双臂机器人系统，利用相位感知模仿学习来自动化和个性化专家策略，以实现精确的双臂协调。
summary_zh: 模仿学习在实现复杂机器人操作自动化方面展现出巨大潜力。在医疗机器人领域，超声引导下的穿刺操作需要精确的双臂协同，因为临床医生必须同时操控超声探头以保持最佳声学视野，同时引导介入针的走向。实现这种非对称工作流程的自动化——并将专家策略可靠地迁移至机器人——仍面临重大挑战。本文提出双臂介入手术系统（DAISS），该遥操作平台能采集高保真双臂示范数据，并学习适用于超声引导介入的相位感知模仿策略。为避免限制操作者的自然行为，DAISS采用基于NDI的灵活主端接口来遥操作两个协同从端机械臂。为支持实时超声反馈下的鲁棒执行，我们开发了一种轻量级、数据高效的模仿策略：该策略采用相位感知架构和针对非对称双臂控制设计的动态掩码损失函数。基于预设轨迹，该网络将实时超声数据与外部视觉观测相融合，生成平滑协调的双臂运动。实验结果表明，DAISS能够从有限示范中学习个性化专家策略。这些发现凸显了相位感知模仿学习驱动的双臂机器人在提升图像引导介入精度、降低认知负荷方面的应用前景。

title: ACCURATE: Arbitrary-shaped Continuum Reconstruction Under Robust Adaptive Two-view Estimation
summary: Accurate reconstruction of arbitrary-shaped long slender continuum bodies, such as guidewires, catheters and other soft continuum manipulators, is essential for accurate mechanical simulation. However, existing image-based reconstruction approaches often suffer from limited accuracy because they often underutilize camera geometry, or lack generality as they rely on rigid geometric assumptions that may fail for continuum robots with complex and highly deformable shapes. To address these limitations, we propose ACCURATE, a 3D reconstruction framework integrating an image segmentation neural network with a geometry-constrained topology traversal and dynamic programming algorithm that enforces global biplanar geometric consistency, minimizes the cumulative point-to-epipolar-line distance, and remains robust to occlusions and epipolar ambiguities cases caused by noise and discretization. Our method achieves high reconstruction accuracy on both simulated and real phantom datasets acquired using a clinical X-ray C-arm system, with mean absolute errors below 1.0 mm.
tldr: The paper introduces ACCURATE, a 3D reconstruction framework for arbitrary-shaped continuum bodies like guidewires and catheters, combining neural network segmentation with geometry-constrained algorithms to improve accuracy and robustness under occlusions and noise.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了ACCURATE，一种用于导丝和导管等任意形状连续体的3D重建框架，结合了神经网络分割与几何约束算法，以提高在遮挡和噪声情况下的准确性和鲁棒性。
summary_zh:
摘要：
精确重建任意形状的细长连续体（如导丝、导管及其他柔性连续体操作器械）对于力学仿真的准确性至关重要。然而现有基于图像的重建方法常因未能充分利用相机几何信息而精度受限，或由于依赖刚性几何假设而缺乏普适性——这类假设对具有复杂高变形特性的连续体机器人往往失效。针对这些局限性，我们提出ACCURATE框架，该三维重建系统将图像分割神经网络与几何约束拓扑遍历及动态规划算法相结合：通过强制全局双平面几何一致性、最小化累计点到极线距离，在噪声和离散化导致的遮挡及极线模糊情况下仍保持鲁棒性。基于临床X射线C型臂系统获取的仿真与实体 phantom 数据集测试表明，本方法平均绝对误差小于1.0毫米，实现了高精度重建。

title: Cable-driven Continuum Robotics: Proprioception via Proximal-integrated Force Sensing
summary: Micro-scale continuum robots face significant limitations in achieving three-dimensional contact force perception, primarily due to structural miniaturization, nonlinear mechanical, and sensor integration. To overcome these limitations, this paper introduces a novel proprioception method for cable-driven continuum robots based on proximal-integrated force sensing (i.e., cable tension and six-axis force/torque (F/T) sensor), inspired by the tendon-joint collaborative sensing mechanism of the finger. By integrating biomechanically inspired design principles with nonlinear modeling, the proposed method addresses the challenge of force perception (including the three-dimensional contact force and the location of the contact point) and shape estimation in micro-scale continuum robots. First, a quasi-bionic mapping between human tissues/organs and robot components is established, enabling the transfer of the integrated sensing strategy of tendons, joints, and neural feedback to the robotic system. Second, a multimodal perception strategy is developed based on the structural constraints inherent to continuum robots. The complex relationships among mechanical and material nonlinearities, robot motion states, and contact forces are formulated as an optimization problem to reduce the perception complexity. Finally, experimental validation demonstrates the effectiveness of the proposed method. This work lays the foundation for developing safer and smarter continuum robots, enabling broader clinical adoption in complex environments.
tldr: This paper introduces a novel proprioception method for cable-driven continuum robots using proximal-integrated force sensing, inspired by human tendon-joint sensing, to achieve 3D contact force perception and shape estimation.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于近端集成力感知（电缆张力和六轴力/扭矩传感器）的新型本体感知方法，用于电缆驱动连续体机器人，灵感来源于人类肌腱-关节协同感知机制，以实现三维接触力感知和形状估计。
summary_zh:
摘要：
微型连续体机器人在实现三维接触力感知方面面临显著局限，主要源于结构微型化、非线性力学特性及传感器集成难题。受手指肌腱-关节协同感知机制启发，本文提出一种基于近端集成力感知（即缆绳张力与六维力/力矩传感器）的缆驱连续体机器人本体感知新方法。通过融合仿生学设计原理与非线性建模，该方法解决了微型连续体机器人力感知（包括三维接触力与接触点定位）与形态估计的挑战。首先，建立人体组织器官与机器人部件的准仿生映射，将肌腱、关节与神经反馈的集成感知策略迁移至机器人系统；其次，基于连续体机器人固有结构约束开发多模态感知策略，将力学与材料非线性、机器人运动状态及接触力间的复杂关系表述为优化问题以降低感知复杂度；最后，实验验证了所提方法的有效性。该研究为开发更安全、更智能的连续体机器人奠定基础，推动其在复杂环境中的临床普及应用。

title: PanoDP: Learning Collision-Free Navigation with Panoramic Depth and Differentiable Physics
summary: Autonomous collision-free navigation in cluttered environments requires safe decision-making under partial observability with both static structure and dynamic obstacles. We present \textbf{PanoDP}, a communication-free learning framework that combines four-view panoramic depth perception with differentiable-physics-based training signals. PanoDP encodes panoramic depth using a lightweight CNN and optimizes policies with dense differentiable collision and motion-feasibility terms, improving training stability beyond sparse terminal collisions. We evaluate PanoDP on a controlled ring-to-center benchmark with systematic sweeps over agent count, obstacle density/layout, and dynamic behaviors, and further test out-of-distribution generalization in an external simulator (e.g., AirSim). Across settings, PanoDP increases collision-free and completion rates over single-view and non-physics-guided baselines under matched training budgets, and ablations (view masking, rotation augmentation) confirm the policy leverages 360-degree information. Code will be open source upon acceptance.
tldr: PanoDP is a learning framework for autonomous collision-free navigation that combines panoramic depth perception with differentiable physics, improving training stability and performance in cluttered environments.
tldr_zh: PanoDP 是一个结合全景深度感知和可微分物理的学习框架，用于自主无碰撞导航，在杂乱环境中提高了训练稳定性和性能。
summary_zh:
摘要：
在复杂环境中实现自主无碰撞导航，需在静态结构与动态障碍物并存的部分可观测条件下进行安全决策。本文提出\textbf{PanoDP}——一种无需通信的学习框架，该框架将四视角全景深度感知与基于可微分物理的训练信号相结合。PanoDP采用轻量级CNN编码全景深度信息，并通过密集可微分碰撞项与运动可行性项优化策略，从而提升训练稳定性，避免仅依赖稀疏终端碰撞信号的问题。我们在受控环形至中心基准测试中系统评估了智能体数量、障碍物密度/布局及动态行为的影响，并进一步在外部仿真器（如AirSim）中测试分布外泛化能力。实验表明，在相同训练成本下，PanoDP相比单视角和非物理引导基线方法显著提高了无碰撞率和任务完成率，消融实验（视角掩蔽、旋转增强）证实策略有效利用了360度全景信息。代码将在论文录用后开源。

title: A Primer on Evolutionary Frameworks for Near-Field Multi-Source Localization
summary: This paper introduces a novel class of model-driven evolutionary frameworks for near-field multi-source localization, addressing the major limitations of grid-based subspace methods such as MUSIC and data-dependent deep learning approaches. To this end, we develop two complementary evolutionary localization frameworks that operate directly on the continuous spherical-wave signal model and support arbitrary array geometries without requiring labeled data, discretized angle--range grids, or architectural constraints. The first framework, termed NEar-field MultimOdal DE (NEMO-DE) associates each individual in the evolutionary population to a single source and optimizes a residual least-squares objective in a sequential manner, updating the data residual and enforcing spatial separation to estimate multiple source locations. To overcome the limitation of NEMO-DE under large power imbalances among the sources, we propose the second framework, named NEar-field Eigen-subspace Fitting DE (NEEF-DE), which jointly encodes all source locations and minimizes a subspace-fitting criterion that aligns a model-based array response subspace with the received signal subspace. Although the proposed frameworks are algorithm-agnostic and compatible with various evolutionary optimizers, differential evolution (DE) is adopted in this work as a representative search strategy due to its simplicity, robustness, and strong empirical performance. We provide extensive numerical experiments to evaluate the performance of the proposed frameworks under different system configurations. This work establishes evolutionary computation as a powerful and flexible paradigm for model-based near-field localization, paving the way for future innovations in this domain.
tldr: The paper introduces two evolutionary frameworks, NEMO-DE and NEEF-DE, for near-field multi-source localization, addressing limitations of traditional methods like MUSIC and deep learning by operating directly on continuous signal models without requiring labeled data or grid discretization.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了两种进化框架，NEMO-DE和NEEF-DE，用于近场多源定位，通过直接在连续信号模型上操作，无需标记数据或网格离散化，解决了传统方法（如MUSIC）和深度学习的局限性。
summary_zh: 本文提出了一类新型的模型驱动进化计算框架，用于解决近场多源定位问题，克服了基于网格的子空间方法（如MUSIC）和数据依赖型深度学习方法的主要局限性。为此，我们开发了两个互补的进化定位框架：这些框架直接基于连续球面波信号模型运行，支持任意阵列构型，且无需标记数据、离散化角度-距离网格或架构约束。首个框架称为近场多模态差分进化（NEMO-DE），将进化群体中的每个个体关联至单一信源，通过顺序优化残差最小二乘目标函数，更新数据残差并强制空间分离以实现多信源定位。针对NEMO-DE在信源间大功率失衡场景下的局限，我们提出第二个框架——近场本征子空间拟合差分进化（NEEF-DE），该框架联合编码所有信源位置，通过最小化子空间拟合准则，使基于模型的阵列响应子空间与接收信号子空间对齐。虽然所提框架具有算法无关性且兼容多种进化优化器，但本研究采用差分进化（DE）作为代表性搜索策略，因其具有简洁性、鲁棒性和优异的实证性能。我们通过大量数值实验评估了所提框架在不同系统配置下的性能。本工作确立了进化计算作为模型驱动近场定位的强大灵活范式，为该领域的未来创新奠定了基础。

title: Online Tracking with Predictions for Nonlinear Systems with Koopman Linear Embedding
summary: We study the problem of online tracking in unknown nonlinear dynamical systems, where only short-horizon predictions of future target states are available. This setting arises in practical scenarios where full future information and exact system dynamics are unavailable. We focus on a class of nonlinear systems that admit a Koopman linear embedding, enabling the dynamics to evolve linearly in a lifted space. Exploiting this structure, we analyze a model-free predictive tracking algorithm based on Willems' fundamental lemma, which imposes dynamic constraints using only past data within a receding-horizon control framework. We show that, for Koopman-linearizable systems, the cumulative cost and dynamic regret of the nonlinear tracking problem coincide with those of the lifted linear counterpart. Moreover, we prove that the dynamic regret of our algorithm decays exponentially with the prediction horizon, as validated by numerical experiments.
tldr: The paper presents a model-free predictive tracking algorithm for nonlinear systems using Koopman linear embedding, showing that the cumulative cost and dynamic regret align with the lifted linear counterpart and decay exponentially with the prediction horizon.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种基于Koopman线性嵌入的非线性系统模型自由预测跟踪算法，表明累积成本和动态遗憾与提升线性对应物一致，并随预测范围呈指数衰减。
summary_zh:
摘要：
本文研究了未知非线性动态系统中的在线跟踪问题，其中仅能获取目标状态短时域的预测信息。该场景适用于实际应用中无法获得完整未来信息及精确系统动力学模型的情况。我们聚焦于一类允许Koopman线性嵌入的非线性系统，其动力学可在提升空间中呈现线性演化。利用这一特性，我们分析了基于Willems基本引理的无模型预测跟踪算法，该算法在滚动时域控制框架下仅利用历史数据施加动态约束。研究表明，对于可Koopman线性化的系统，非线性跟踪问题的累积成本与动态遗憾值均与其提升后的线性系统对应量一致。此外，我们证明该算法的动态遗憾值随预测时域呈指数级衰减，数值实验验证了这一结论。
